seniority.level: Director / Group PM (people manager of PMs)
level_signals: Leading and growing a team of experienced Product Managers

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years of managing product managers, 8+ years as a product manager
3+ years of managing product managers, 8+ years as a product manager
Deep user empathy and a high bar for user experience
Experience developing a multi-year vision, strategy and roadmap for large scale products
Experience hiring, managing, and building high performing customer-focused Product Management teams in multiple continents
Experience leading cross-team initiatives from ideation to execution
Technical expertise: You've led technical products and/or have a background in computer science (or similar)
Strong written and verbal communication skills

scope.surface: Conversational AI and human support experience platform across Stripe's product suite
primary_metrics_inferred: Scale of customers served (millions of businesses)
core_responsibilities: Own and drive the product roadmap for the future of conversational AI and human support at Stripe
Shape strategy, steer product requirements, and hold a high bar for the execution of high impact initiatives that impact key segments and top customers
Partner with Stripe's AI teams to unlock opportunities for serving users more effectively
Build strong relationships with senior leaders across Operations to continually uplevel our human support functions
Grow and coach a global team of PMs in building systems and platforms for conversational and human agents

green_flags: High-visibility area within Stripe with direct exposure to company leadership
Explicit encouragement to apply if minimum requirements are met
Clear product surface: conversational AI + human support at scale
Opportunity to work at the forefront of applied AI
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid policy stated anywhere in the JD — candidates cannot assess logistics
Preferred qualifications section is referenced but entirely empty — no nice-to-haves surfaced

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirements + people-manager signals; not explicitly titled)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
ambiguities: Technical expertise requirement uses 'and/or' — either leading technical products OR a CS background satisfies it; treated as a single hard requirement with both paths acceptable.

Preferred qualifications are mentioned in the JD structure ('The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement') but no preferred qualifications are actually listed; nice_to_haves is therefore empty.
Location is entirely absent from the JD body — it is unclear whether this role is remote, hybrid, or onsite, and in which geography.
